rationale: Y directly engages the three real GT threads: it validates/explains the exact cudagraph_mode-in-modeling question gcanlin raised and the TODO resolving it, explains the seed-reproducibility tradeoff gcanlin flagged, explicitly asks for the before/after benchmark evidence multiple reviewers demanded, and questions the NPU compilation_config fix tied to Wallbreazzz's crash report. X instead spends its 'major' findings on generic 'add a unit test' asks that no GT reviewer raised, only weakly brushing against the TODO/benchmark topics. Both correctly flag the stale/contradictory YAML comments and the redundant omni_pooler_payload_include_hidden line, but Y's findings are more substantively grounded in verified runner/config code paths, giving it better precision alongside much higher recall.
